what_it_does: A fast read-only census of the Company backend-core region (runtime, store, fabric, contracts, plus root markdown and sampled voice/ops/nodes/roles), listing each file's apparent purpose, a deep-read priority marker, and notes drawn from docstrings/headings without logic tracing. Produces a directory tree, per-region file tables, a summary, a top-10 deep-read priority list, and notable observations.
observations: Renders an ASCII directory tree of the whole repo with byte sizes for root docs and one-line purposes for each module file.
Provides per-region file tables (runtime 13 modules, store 2, fabric 4, contracts 10, root markdown 8) each with appears-to-be / PRIORITY / notes columns.

Marks suite.py double-star (the heart: scheduler spine, RHM dispatch, voice, self-mod governance) and bridge.py, registry.py, scheduler.py, governance.py, fs_store.py, address.py, node_record.py, node_type.py, bridge_msgs.py, client.py as priority deep-reads.
Maps contract files to their C-numbers (address.py=C1, node_type.py=C2, node_record.py=C3, resolver.py=C4, bridge_msgs.py=C8, tools.py=C7) and notes ui_info.py carries 77 addressed elements.
Gives a top-10 deep-read priority list led by suite.py, bridge.py, fs_store.py, address.py, node_record.py.

Lists notable observations (self-describing repo, cross-process fcntl + RLock + fsync concurrency, schema-additive Pydantic, live governance postures, no hardcoding, first-real-use is its own codebase) and a not-yet-scanned next-pass list.
standouts: The C-number mapping of contract files anchors the abstract contract specs to concrete source files.
Identification of suite.py and bridge.py as the two faces meeting at one Suite, and fs_store.py as the durability/concurrency keystone.
Self-flags scope honestly: docstring/heading-only, no logic trace, canvas/nodes/roles/tests not deep-scanned this pass.
conventions: Carries frontmatter (type: inventory, region, scope, date) per the vault convention.
Uses a consistent star-based PRIORITY notation and appears-to-be hedging language appropriate to a no-logic-trace census.
Explicitly separates scanned from not-yet-scanned regions, matching the repo's coverage-honesty discipline.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the backend-core file map, the fastest way to learn what every runtime/store/fabric/contracts file is and which ten matter most for a deep read.
questions: The note that build-prep vault specs may not be accessible from a WSL agent (Windows-side) raises whether that source was actually reachable for the deep reads.
Are the file sizes and module list still current, or has the backend grown since 2026-06-08?
purpose_vs_actual: Match. Stated as a fast read-only structure+purpose census marked for priority deep-read, and it delivers a tree, tables, priorities, and honest scope flags.
apparent_themes: Contracts as the pinned seams of the system (C1-C8 anchored to files)
The self-describing, registry-driven, schema-additive backend as already-disciplined terrain
intention_signals: Trying to give the deeper research layer a complete, honestly-scoped inventory of the backend-core files with priority markers, so the expensive deep-read passes know exactly which files to open and why.
contribution: Contributes the backend-core file-by-file inventory with C-number anchoring and deep-read priorities; a fused system keeps this as the structural index of the runtime/store/fabric/contracts region that points to the files the deep reads characterize.
summary_for_embedding: A 2026-06-08 fast read-only census of the Company backend core (runtime/store/fabric/contracts + root docs) with a directory tree, per-file purpose tables, C-number contract anchoring, deep-read priority markers, and a top-10 priority list.
intention_for_embedding: To give the deep-read research layer an honestly-scoped inventory of backend-core files with priorities so it knows which files to open and why.
